workflows-reference.purpose: Provide workflows for selecting and applying an evidence-based architecture orientation.

documentation-context.rule: Read docs/architecture/research-based-architecture.md, docs/foundation/researched-principles.md, and docs/process/researched-workflow.md before planning.
Use current primary or authoritative sources when the selected direction is not yet locked.
Cite sources in decision docs before implementation depends on the researched choice.

task-memory-gate.rule: For long or risky work with at least two concrete implementation slices after alignment or design, load Compass references/task-memory.xml.
Implementation may not start until the task memory outcome is reported as created, resumed, or not-required.
When task memory is required, docs/.tasks/<task>/goal.md, diagram.md, and memories.md must exist before the first implementation edit or command.
Missing docs/_templates task memory files are a documentation gap, not permission to skip this gate.

workflow.description: Add one capability only after research has selected the architecture, principles, and workflow to apply.

enforcement.rule: Run phases sequentially: research lock, design, implementation.
Do not implement feature code until the researched orientation is selected and recorded.
Research must compare at least three viable combinations when the direction is unknown.
Implementation follows the workflow recorded by the research decision.
Before implementation starts, satisfy the task-memory-gate for any long or risky multi-slice feature.

phase.name: research-lock
phase.purpose: Select the orientation from current evidence before feature design.

actions.action: Read existing docs and inspect the codebase.
Browse current primary or authoritative sources for architecture, principles, and workflow options.
Compare at least three viable combinations.
Recommend one direction with trade-offs and source links.
Update research-based architecture, principles, workflow, and decision docs after approval.

phase.name: design
phase.purpose: Design the feature according to the approved researched orientation.

actions.action: Translate the researched orientation into concrete boundaries, tests, and docs for the selected feature.
Define behavior contract, ownership, data flow, and verification strategy.
Mark behavior selected, rejected, or postponed.

phase-output.orientation-application: How the researched direction shapes this feature.

phase.name: implementation
phase.purpose: Implement the approved feature under the researched orientation.

actions.action: Before the first implementation edit or command, report the task memory outcome: created, resumed, or not-required.
Implement one verified slice at a time.
Verify behavior and any assumptions from the researched architecture.

workflow.evidence: Approved research lock, cited sources, approved design, and implementation verification.

steps.step: State the research question and decision to be unlocked.
Gather current primary or authoritative sources.
Compare at least three options when selecting orientation.
Update research docs or decision records when the user approves.

workflow.evidence: Sources, comparison, recommendation, and documented trade-offs.
